affects: all EN pages: index, daily, question, question-draw, reading, full, profile
author: dunkin-novice
filed: Phase 2 originally bundled :root token overrides (--color-bg, --panel, etc.); deferred after Step 0 discovery surfaced cascade conflicts on .primary, .btn-primary, .card .back, .result-card. Token flips belong with Phase 4 (results) and Phase 5 (daily board) component refactors.

EN/TH parity intentionally not claimed: Thai pages (/th/*) deliberately not linked phase-2-header.css. Thai font rendering with Playfair Display serif requires separate validation (Safe Rollout Guide Phase 7).
Phase 2.1 backlog item (clickable header logo) recorded in CLAUDE.md, not started.
goal: Globalize light frosted-glass header to all EN pages by extending Phase 1 header rules out from under the body[data-page=home] scope.
verified: per-page link tag insertion confirmed via grep across all 7 EN pages
new file contains zero body[data-page=home] selectors (header is unscoped, intentional)
cascade order on index.html: theme-tokens -> phase-2-header -> phase-1-home

affects: all EN pages on mobile widths <=768px where bottom-nav is active
author: dunkin-novice
filed: Bottom-nav bar itself remains dark navy on mobile alongside the now-light header. Recorded in CLAUDE.md Backlog as a separate visual decision (legitimate iOS-native pattern vs full pastel consistency).

Specificity-tie pattern documented in commit message body for Phases 3-7 to avoid: dropping body[data-page=X] scope without compensating for bottom-nav.css selector specificity will reproduce this bug.
goal: Fix mobile dark-header bug introduced by Phase 2 by overriding the higher-specificity body.has-bottom-nav .site-header rule from bottom-nav.css.
verified: real iPhone Safari at 393px confirmed the bug existed pre-patch
cascade analysis traced the conflict to bottom-nav.css line 83 at specificity (0,2,1) beating Phase 2 plain .site-header at (0,1,0)
patch matches specificity at (0,2,1) and wins on source order since phase-2-header.css loads after bottom-nav.css (@import-ed at top of styles.css)
only background and border-bottom-color overridden; bottom-nav compact padding preserved
Phase 1 unaffected because body[data-page=home] .site-header ties bottom-nav and wins on source order
